text.p: No qualms Oga! I think that tomorrow evening will be the best option as I also will be quite busy tomorrow morning. Tomorrow, Tuesday, we've arranged an unofficial Bible group meeting with & 's family coming over for a christmas party ( just as we used to have the tradition) at our place. So, as soon as we are done at about 9:30pm, then I'll log-in to skype and we could then chat. I hope tha's ok with you.
Regards from all of us here. Remain in His love! And until tomorrow,
bye.
Oga,

p.other-language: I beg no vex o! I been dey think say I go get time sidon write
you abi find time for skype make we talk, but i be like say time just no gree wait for me..... please make you take God mecy pardon me o!

text.p: Considering your request I have no objections. Please go ahead and use the e-mails from me for your written data. And as for the spoken data we might just fix an appropriate time to meet on skype and get talking. You are free to use the recordings for your research works with pleasure. I will also try to check out the materials I have on Cds or mp3 files and get them across to you as soon as possible. Is there a deadline....?
Looking forward to hearing from you!
God bless! Bye

Oga, Compliments of the seasons in the unfailing grace and love of our Saviour Jesus Christ. Sorry indeed that I have not been able to write for w few weeks now. In fact I wanted to reply immeiately to your last mail - in which you forwarded a message, but as it turned out I haven't been able until now.. First of all, thanks for that Word. It was a true blessing.

By his grace we are all doing fine and the Lord has been faithfull. All is well. It's just that god has poured such wonderful showrs of blessings upon us, such that we are simply trusting Him for grace to cathc up with time.... but we are truely grateful our Papa wey dey for Heaven Yonder... Im blessing na waya o!
We are getting adjusted. The Fellowship is going on LIVE! We meet as usual and believe God for other men of like passions. Please keep on praying for us. greetings from all of us.

I forgot to inform you earlier in my previous mails that I have collected the things you kept for me at 's place. Thank you very much indeed. And we have decided to give 800 zl for the PC, I hope say we no under-estimate the things. If you agreee to that sum then it stands that you have the money with us.
We are praying for you and trust the Lord to make room for you there. Take care and the Lord richly bless you.
Love from the s
Oga I take all body throway salute,

We are doing very fine by the grace of God and we all send our greetings. The church is also fine. I will communicate your love to them all. However, this weekend we are travelling to my in-laws to greet them.
Concerning the new developments on my job... God has been incredibly faithful. So many testimonies already and much more on the way, because God is still at work with us, in us and through us.

As soon as I made up my mind to resign my job I started to take seriously the intension to do a professional career course in an English speaking country - fortunately we had met someone from the USA during one of the missionary outreaches we had in the South of Poland and so far he has been making contacts with his acquaintances and friends who are Veterinarians there to see the possibility of my plans. We have been praying in that respect that God would make a way... so that is a plan in the pipeline.

Next step was to consider earning a livelihood for 'survival' while I am thinking up solutions for one professional work or the other. So I contacted an acquaintance and I found out that there was still the vacancy you left at SOCRATES Language School in . I approached the Lady and she wanted to hire me but on a job agreement that excludes welfare insurances i.e. umowa odzelo - well for me that wasn't good enough because it means a discontinuity of my previous insurances. We decided with my wife and concluded it was better to set up our own company so that it enables me to pay my own insurrances, which means that the employer is bound by law to pay me a full BRUTTO ( gross) salary and I am responsible to continue the payment of my own insurrances. This was a good decision but we couldn't start a company together as a couple since at that moment one of us wasnt employed.

So I decided to start the company in my own name. We went to the Immigration office to check the laws and they said I was qualified to have a private company since I had my Karta Osiedlenie ( i.e. Permanent residency permit), although I still needed NOSTRIFICATION and CITIZENSHIP to be able to start a private veterinary clinic, so I have started a language school called CENTRUM JEZYKOWE "GO 4 IT". The final documentations are underway by His grace and we are finding favour all the way. Now I have a lot of prospective students already. So what this means is that I could work at Socrates in the evenings and still have a few free hours with private students. And I could present my employer or clients with invoices. But the goodnews still remain my brother. According to Polish Law I, as a first-timer company owner, am entitled to pay only 1/3 of insurrances for the first 2 years of the life of the company. Goodnews indeed as that also maximamises my profit and capital investment. It's a new leaf the Lord has turned for me in Poland.

In order not to lose contact with my profession while I am planning for the future and waiting for my nostrification I have decided to consider any small veterinary practice where I could just keep in touch with the profession and also to be sure that whenever I will require a testimonial of practice I could ask a doctor to sign on my behalf. Sometimes it is required that one has a minimum requiremnet of practice experience in the country of intended nostrification. So to this effect I contacted a clinic and am due for an interview on Monday at 10 am. Please pray also on my behalf for favour.

On the spiritual front, similarly, God has been doing wonders. We have started meeting in Cafe Betty on Saturdays @ 4 pm. Our website is already under construction and God is giving us great ideas. We trust Him. And as the development progresses you shall be informed. Right now we, indeed and more than ever, covet your prayers on all fronts.
Please find attached, a few files of the materials you requested for and others will come by post to the address you sent as soon as possible.
We love you and are praying for you as well. God is indeed faithful. Our regards also to your family back home. Shalom!!!!!!!!

Oga we kamkpe, God dey bless us yanfu- yanfu. By God's grace I will collect all the necessary material and send them to you. But I've just realized that it might be better by post since the files may be to too heavy to go through the web via e-mail. In that case, you may have to send me your contact postal address as soon as possible.

The latest news, however, is that I decided to resign from my job at the veterinary clinic. I could no longer bare with their crooked decisions and ways of doing things and moreso I was convinced God wanted me to put in my resignation. For now I am looking up to God for new possibilities and I trust that His help is right on the way.
We are fine and doing well by His grace. Our love to you and we wish you all the best out there. God is with you and He will surely make room for you in Germany.
Remain blessed and focussed.
Maranatha!!!
The s
My Oga,

Thank you very much for your mail.We have more or less been on the road for most of the last 3 weeks just having a few days interval at work and then back on the roads again. At the moment we are in Beskida South of Poland working with some American Missionaries out there reaching out to the Roma people (Gypsie as they are more often known), working as translators.

All of us dey forward march our greeting come your side for yonder there... my wife & children all dey salute you yanfu yanfu! We dey miss you too and constant constant we dey pray God make Him take sweep comot for road every doti wey dey for road as you do the documentation. God dey your side powerfully.As you settle down i go fine! Isaiah 55;12

By His grace we are trusting the Lord for a more concrete work on the vision. As I mentioned earlier concerning the suituable venue, as soon as we return to Poznan I will make sure that it's ironed out. Although I have talked with the daughter of the owner of the restaurant we talked about and hopefully we might be given access to the place even on saturdays though they fuction only on Mon-Fri.

Since our last meeting together with we also haven't met at all, just been in touch by telephone. She took a few days of holiday and has been travelling around Poland especially at the weekends such that for a while now she hasn't been in church services. So I hope to talk with her as soon as we all get back to Poznan.
Beloved we are praying for you and trust to Lord for all the best. Take care and our best regards also to your family back home in Nigeria. We love you dearly.
Thanks also for the remider about the PC documents from your neighbour, I'll do that as soon we get back. Take care.
From all the s
Poland.
